chapter_summary: Jesus sends out 72 disciples with authority and they return with joy. He gives thanks to the Father for revealing truth to little children. A lawyer asks 'Who is my neighbour?' and Jesus answers with the story of the Good Samaritan — a man who crossed every boundary to help a stranger. Mary and Martha receive Jesus, and Mary chooses what cannot be taken away: sitting at His feet.
main_lesson: Your neighbour is anyone who needs your help. Following Jesus means crossing the road to care for people — not walking past. And the best thing you can do, like Mary, is to sit and listen to Jesus.

memory_verse.kjv: And he answering said, Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy strength, and with all thy mind; and thy neighbour as thyself.
memory_verse.little_bible: Love God with everything you have — your heart, your soul, your strength, and your mind. And love the people around you as much as you love yourself.

parent_guide: Luke 10 is one of the most ethically and theologically rich chapters in Luke. The sending of the 72 (v1-20) shows the expanding mission — not just the 12 but a larger company are sent. Jesus' prayer (v21-22) is extraordinary — He thanks the Father that the things hidden from 'the wise' are revealed to 'little children' (nepiois). This is one of the most encouraging verses for parents teaching young children: Jesus says children can receive revelation adults miss. The Good Samaritan (v25-37) is the answer to 'who is my neighbour' — note that the question was asked to justify limiting love ('who MUST I love?'); Jesus' counter-question at the end flips it ('who proved to be a neighbour?'). The Samaritan was the one considered unclean by the lawyer — yet he is the one who shows mercy. Martha and Mary (v38-42): the 'one thing needed' is presence with Jesus over performance for Jesus. Both have their place, but when we are anxious and troubled by many things, the priority is still listening.
application_for_children: Jesus says YOUR neighbour is anyone who needs your help — even a stranger! When you see someone who is hurt, left out, or needs kindness, that is your moment to be like the Good Samaritan. And like Mary, the best thing we can do is stop and listen to Jesus.

verses.kjv: After these things the Lord appointed other seventy also, and sent them two and two before his face into every city and place, whither he himself would come.
verses.little_bible: After this, Jesus chose 72 more followers and sent them out in pairs to every town and village He was planning to visit. They were going ahead to get things ready.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus sent 72 helpers out in pairs to every town He was going to visit.
verses.meaning: Jesus expanded His mission beyond the 12 apostles. By sending people two by two, He ensured they had a companion and could confirm each other's message.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus sends us out together.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for not sending me alone. Help me work with others for You.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Jesus sent the disciples in pairs instead of alone?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus was preparing towns before He arrived. How can your family prepare your heart and home to welcome Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Do something kind with a friend or sibling today — work together for good.
verses.illustration_prompt: Pairs of cheerful disciples walking along dusty roads toward distant villages at sunrise, sandals on their feet, cloaks on their backs. Soft golden morning light, storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: Therefore said he unto them, The harvest truly is great, but the labourers are few: pray ye therefore the Lord of the harvest, that he would send forth labourers into his harvest.
verses.little_bible: Jesus told them: 'There are so many people ready to hear about God — it is like a huge harvest field! But there are not enough workers. Pray and ask God to send more workers into His harvest.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'There are so many people to reach for God. Pray for more helpers!'
verses.meaning: Jesus saw the world as a ripe harvest — people ready to receive God's love. The problem was never lack of need but lack of willing workers.
verses.memory_phrase: Pray for more workers for God.
verses.prayer: God, You see so many people who need to hear about You. Please send more workers — and help me be one of them.
verses.discussion_question: What is a harvest? What do farmers have to do at harvest time?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus says to pray for labourers. Who in your life could you pray would become someone who tells others about God?
verses.do_it_today: Pray today for one person who does not yet know Jesus — ask God to send someone to tell them.
verses.illustration_prompt: A vast golden wheat field stretching to the horizon, only a few small figures harvesting, and a wide sky above. Simple, warm, storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Go your ways: behold, I send you forth as lambs among wolves.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'Now go! But I am sending you out like little lambs into a place where there are wolves. You will need to be brave and trust Me.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Go out — but be brave. You are like little lambs among wolves.'
verses.meaning: Jesus did not hide the danger — following Him can mean facing hostility. Yet He still sent them, showing that the mission is worth the risk.
verses.memory_phrase: Be brave — Jesus sends you.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me be brave when following You feels scary. You are with me.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever had to do something brave? How did you feel before and after?
verses.family_discussion: Why do you think Jesus told them about the danger instead of hiding it? What does that say about honest faith?
verses.do_it_today: Do one brave thing for Jesus today — tell someone you believe in Him.
verses.illustration_prompt: A small fluffy lamb stepping bravely onto a wide road, with shadowy forest on either side. Warm light ahead. Gentle watercolour storybook style.

verses.kjv: Carry neither purse, nor scrip, nor shoes: and salute no man by the way.
verses.little_bible: Jesus told them: 'Don't bring a money bag, a travel bag, or extra sandals. And don't stop to chat along the way — this mission is urgent!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Don't pack bags or extra shoes. This is an urgent trip — keep going!'
verses.meaning: The disciples were to travel light and fast, depending entirely on God's provision. The urgency of the message meant no time for lengthy distractions.
verses.memory_phrase: Travel light and trust God.
verses.prayer: God, help me not be weighed down by things I don't need. Help me trust You to provide.
verses.discussion_question: If you had to travel somewhere with only what you could carry, what would you bring?
verses.family_discussion: What 'bags' does your family carry — worries, busyness, distractions — that might slow you down from following Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Put aside one distraction today so you can focus on something that matters for God.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two disciples walking quickly down a dusty road, hands empty, sandals on, not pausing to look at anything around them. Purposeful strides. Bright road ahead.

verses.kjv: And into whatsoever house ye enter, first say, Peace be to this house.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'When you enter someone's house, the first words out of your mouth should be: "Peace be to this house!"'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'When you enter a home, say: Peace be to this house!'
verses.meaning: Peace was not just a greeting — it was a blessing. The disciples were carriers of God's peace, and their arrival should bring it.
verses.memory_phrase: Carry peace wherever you go.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me carry Your peace into every room I walk into today.
verses.discussion_question: What does peace feel like? Can you make a place feel more peaceful by the way you act?
verses.family_discussion: How can your family be bringers of peace — at home, at school, with friends?
verses.do_it_today: When you walk into a room today, try to make it more peaceful by your attitude and words.
verses.illustration_prompt: A disciple standing at the doorway of a simple stone home, hand raised in greeting, face kind and warm, a family inside looking up with interest. Soft afternoon light.

verses.kjv: And if the son of peace be there, your peace shall rest upon it: if not, it shall turn to you again.
verses.little_bible: Jesus explained: 'If the people in that house are ready to receive peace, your blessing will stay with them. If they are not ready, your peace will come back to you — it will not be wasted.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: If they welcome peace, it stays. If not, your blessing comes back to you.
verses.meaning: God's peace is not wasted even when rejected. It returns to the one who offered it. We cannot control others' responses, only our own faithfulness.
verses.memory_phrase: God's peace is never wasted.
verses.prayer: God, help me offer kindness and peace even when people don't receive it. You keep the blessing safe.
verses.discussion_question: Has someone ever been unkind when you tried to be kind? How did that feel?
verses.family_discussion: Why is it important to keep offering love and peace even when people don't seem to want it?
verses.do_it_today: Be kind to someone today even if you think they might not notice or say thank you.
verses.illustration_prompt: A disciple offering a gentle hand of blessing at a doorstep, one home glowing with warmth inside, another remaining dark and closed. Peaceful evening colours.

verses.kjv: And in the same house remain, eating and drinking such things as they give: for the labourer is worthy of his hire. Go not from house to house.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'Stay in that same house and eat and drink whatever they give you — don't be picky. A worker deserves to be looked after. Don't keep moving from house to house.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Stay in one home and eat what they give you. A worker deserves to be cared for.'
verses.meaning: God's workers should be willing to receive hospitality humbly. Contentment and faithfulness — staying put rather than looking for something better — were marks of a trustworthy disciple.
verses.memory_phrase: Be content with what God provides.
verses.prayer: God, help me be thankful for what I have and not always want more.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to be content? Is it easy or hard for you?
verses.family_discussion: How does gratitude for what we have change the way we live? What can your family be more grateful for?
verses.do_it_today: At dinner tonight, thank God specifically for the food in front of you — even if it is simple.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two disciples sitting happily at a family's table, sharing a simple meal of bread and olives, laughing with their hosts. Warm lamplight, humble home, joyful atmosphere.

verses.kjv: And into whatsoever city ye enter, and they receive you, eat such things as are set before you:
verses.little_bible: Jesus continued: 'When you enter a city and the people welcome you, eat whatever they put in front of you.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: When a town welcomes you, eat what they give you — don't complain.
verses.meaning: The disciples were to be humble guests, not demanding ones. The mission required flexibility and gratitude, not fussiness.
verses.memory_phrase: Receive kindness with thankfulness.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me receive what others give me with a thankful heart, not a complaining one.
verses.discussion_question: Is it easy or hard to eat food someone else makes for you, even if it is different from what you are used to?
verses.family_discussion: Hospitality requires both giving and gracious receiving. How can your family practise both?
verses.do_it_today: Eat your food today without complaining, and say thank you to whoever made it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Disciples seated at a townsperson's table, graciously accepting bowls of food being passed to them, warm smiles all around. Village courtyard, evening light.

verses.kjv: And heal the sick that are therein, and say unto them, The kingdom of God is come nigh unto you.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'Heal the sick people in that town, and tell them: "The kingdom of God has come close to you!"'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Heal the sick and tell them: 'God's kingdom has come near!'
verses.meaning: Healing and proclamation went together. The kingdom of God was not just a future hope — it was arriving now, demonstrated in acts of healing and the spoken word.
verses.memory_phrase: God's kingdom is coming near!
verses.prayer: God, let Your kingdom come near to me today — and near to everyone around me.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think 'the kingdom of God' means? What is it like when God is in charge?
verses.family_discussion: How can your family show that God's kingdom has come near — through healing, kindness, and words of truth?
verses.do_it_today: Pray for someone who is sick today. Ask God to heal them.
verses.illustration_prompt: A disciple kneeling beside a sick man on a mat in a courtyard, hands gently placed on his shoulders, the man's eyes opening with hope, neighbours watching in wonder. Warm golden light.

verses.kjv: But into whatsoever city ye enter, and they receive you not, go your ways out into the streets of the same, and say,
verses.little_bible: Jesus also said: 'But if you go into a city and they do not welcome you, go out into the streets and announce this—'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: But if a town does not welcome you, go into the streets and say this:
verses.meaning: Rejection does not silence the message. Jesus prepared His disciples not to be discouraged but to still speak clearly when a town turned them away.
verses.memory_phrase: Keep speaking even when rejected.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me not give up when people don't listen. Help me keep being faithful.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever felt like no one was listening to you? What did you do?
verses.family_discussion: What does it look like to be faithful even when people reject your message? How do we keep going?
verses.do_it_today: If someone did not listen to you yesterday when you tried to do good, try again today with kindness.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two disciples walking out of a closed city gate, heads high, not looking back in shame, stepping into bright open streets. Purposeful, dignified posture.

verses.kjv: Even the very dust of your city, which cleaveth on our feet, we do wipe off against you: notwithstanding be ye sure of this, that the kingdom of God is come nigh unto you.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said they should say: 'We wipe even the dust of your town from our feet as a sign against you. But know this — the kingdom of God was right here, close to you!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The disciples would say: 'We wipe your dust from our feet. But the kingdom of God came close to you!'
verses.meaning: Wiping dust from feet was a solemn sign of separation. Even in rejecting the disciples, the town had not been able to reject the nearness of God's kingdom — and they would be accountable for it.
verses.memory_phrase: God's kingdom was right there.
verses.prayer: God, help me never ignore You when You are close to me. Help me respond to You today.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean that God's kingdom came close to someone who pushed it away?
verses.family_discussion: We all have moments when God is near and we ignore Him. How can your family be more attentive to those moments?
verses.do_it_today: Pause today and ask: 'God, is there something You are saying right now that I have been ignoring?'
verses.illustration_prompt: A disciple shaking dust from his sandal on the edge of a walled village, the gates closed behind him, light glowing on the road ahead. Solemn and clear.

verses.kjv: But I say unto you, that it shall be more tolerable in that day for Sodom, than for that city.
verses.little_bible: Jesus warned: 'I tell you, on the day of judgement it will be worse for that city than for the wicked city of Sodom.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus warned: 'On judgement day, it will be worse for that city than for Sodom.'
verses.meaning: The greater the privilege of hearing the gospel, the greater the responsibility. Sodom never heard Jesus — but these cities did.
verses.memory_phrase: Hearing God's word is a great privilege.
verses.prayer: God, I don't want to ignore what You have given me. Thank You for letting me hear about Jesus.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think it is more serious to say 'no' to Jesus after hearing about Him?
verses.family_discussion: We have access to God's Word more than most people in history. What responsibility does that put on us?
verses.do_it_today: Read one Bible verse today — and really listen to what it says.
verses.illustration_prompt: A solemn image of a city in shadow while a bright road leads past it toward the horizon. A sense of a great opportunity missed. Quiet and serious watercolour.

verses.kjv: Woe unto thee, Chorazin! woe unto thee, Bethsaida! for if the mighty works had been done in Tyre and Sidon, which have been done in you, they had a great while ago repented, sitting in sackcloth and ashes.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'It will be terrible for you, Chorazin! And for you, Bethsaida! Because if the miracles done in your towns had been done in Tyre and Sidon, those people would have changed their ways long ago — sitting in sadness and asking for forgiveness!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus warned Chorazin and Bethsaida: 'Other cities would have repented if they saw what you saw!'
verses.meaning: These towns had seen Jesus' miracles first-hand and still refused to repent. Spiritual privilege without response brings greater accountability.
verses.memory_phrase: Don't ignore what God has shown you.
verses.prayer: God, I have seen and heard so much about You. Help me not harden my heart. Help me truly follow You.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever been taught something important and then not acted on it? What happened?
verses.family_discussion: We have the whole Bible, Sunday school, church — why do we sometimes still not respond to God? What makes hearts hard?
verses.do_it_today: Think of something you know God wants you to change. Take one small step to change it today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A town perched on a hill, bright and busy — but no one looking toward the light on the horizon where Jesus has departed. A sense of missed opportunity in warm ochre tones.

verses.kjv: But it shall be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon at the judgment, than for you.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'It will go better for Tyre and Sidon on the day of judgement than for you.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Tyre and Sidon will have it easier on judgement day than you.'
verses.meaning: God's judgement is fair and proportional. Those who had more light and rejected it bear more responsibility than those who never heard.
verses.memory_phrase: God judges fairly.
verses.prayer: Lord, You are always fair. Help me respond to what You have shown me, not waste it.
verses.discussion_question: Do you think it is fair that people who know more have more responsibility?
verses.family_discussion: How does knowing God's Word change our responsibility to live by it?
verses.do_it_today: Read one thing from the Bible today and ask: 'What does God want me to do with this?'
verses.illustration_prompt: Scales of justice held by an unseen hand, one side with a great stack of scrolls and miracles, tipping heavily. Simple and meaningful watercolour.

verses.kjv: And thou, Capernaum, which art exalted to heaven, shalt be thrust down to hell.
verses.little_bible: Jesus continued: 'And you, Capernaum — you think you have been lifted up to heaven? No! You will be brought down to the depths!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus warned Capernaum: 'You think you are lifted high — but you will be brought very low.'
verses.meaning: Capernaum was Jesus' base of operations and saw more miracles than almost anywhere else. Pride and indifference in the face of such blessing brings the sharpest consequence.
verses.memory_phrase: Pride goes before a fall.
verses.prayer: God, keep me humble. Don't let me take Your blessings for granted or become proud.
verses.discussion_question: What happens when someone becomes proud or thinks they are better than everyone else?
verses.family_discussion: Capernaum had every blessing and still turned away. How do we guard our family against taking God's goodness for granted?
verses.do_it_today: Say to God: 'Thank You for everything You have given me. I don't want to take any of it for granted.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A prosperous city on a hill, lights glowing — but surrounded by clouds that seem to be pressing it down. A sense of pride about to meet humility. Dramatic watercolour tones.

verses.kjv: He that heareth you heareth me; and he that despiseth you despiseth me; and he that despiseth me despiseth him that sent me.
verses.little_bible: Jesus told His disciples: 'Whoever listens to you is listening to Me. And whoever rejects you is rejecting Me — and rejecting Me means rejecting the Father who sent Me.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Whoever hears you hears Me. Whoever rejects you rejects Me and My Father.'
verses.meaning: The disciples carried Jesus' authority. How people responded to them was really a response to Jesus Himself — and to God the Father.
verses.memory_phrase: When we speak for Jesus, He speaks through us.
verses.prayer: Jesus, when I tell others about You, speak through my words. Let them hear You, not just me.
verses.discussion_question: If a king sends a messenger, and you ignore the messenger, what does that say about how you feel about the king?
verses.family_discussion: What does it mean that listening to God's Word is the same as listening to God Himself?
verses.do_it_today: Listen carefully today when someone shares something from the Bible. Jesus is speaking through it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A disciple speaking gently to a small group of listeners, while behind him a faint outline of Jesus stands as if speaking through him. Gentle, luminous watercolour.

verses.kjv: And the seventy returned again with joy, saying, Lord, even the devils are subject unto us through thy name.
verses.little_bible: The 72 disciples came back full of joy! They said: 'Lord, even demons obeyed us when we used Your name!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The 72 came back so happy! 'Lord, even evil spirits obeyed Your name!'
verses.meaning: The disciples discovered that Jesus' name carried real power over evil. Their joy came not from performance but from what God did through them.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus' name has power over all evil.
verses.prayer: Lord Jesus, Your name is powerful! I trust in Your name to protect me and help me.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think they were so excited about the demons obeying? What does that tell you about Jesus' power?
verses.family_discussion: The disciples returned with joy because they saw God's power at work. What has your family seen God do that gives you joy?
verses.do_it_today: Shout or whisper joyfully: 'Jesus' name is powerful!' — and mean it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Seventy-two disciples rushing back along a road toward Jesus, faces beaming, arms waving, robes flying, bright afternoon sky behind them. Joyful and triumphant watercolour.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, I beheld Satan as lightning fall from heaven.
verses.little_bible: Jesus told them: 'I saw it — I watched Satan fall from heaven like a bolt of lightning!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'I saw Satan fall from heaven like lightning!'
verses.meaning: Jesus was speaking of Satan's defeat — not just a moment in history, but the cosmic reality of God's victory. What the disciples saw in their mission was a small picture of a greater war already won.
verses.memory_phrase: Satan is defeated by Jesus.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You have already won! Help me live knowing that evil does not have the last word.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever seen lightning? How fast and powerful does it seem?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus saw Satan's defeat before it fully happened. How does knowing that Jesus wins in the end change how we face hard things?
verses.do_it_today: When something feels scary or wrong today, remember: Jesus has already won.
verses.illustration_prompt: A dramatic night sky with a bolt of lightning plunging downward, Jesus watching from a hilltop with calm authority, eyes bright. Epic but peaceful watercolour.

verses.kjv: Behold, I give unto you power to tread on serpents and scorpions, and over all the power of the enemy: and nothing shall by any means hurt you.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'I have given you authority to walk over snakes and scorpions — and over every power of the enemy. Nothing will hurt you.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'I give you power over the enemy. Nothing can hurt you in My name.'
verses.meaning: Jesus' disciples carry His protection and authority. Serpents and scorpions represent the enemy's power — and Jesus declares it is under His followers' feet.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus' power protects me.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for Your protection. I am safe in Your name.
verses.discussion_question: What is the scariest thing you can think of? Does knowing Jesus protects you help you feel less scared?
verses.family_discussion: What does it mean practically to have authority over the enemy? How do we use that authority in prayer and daily life?
verses.do_it_today: Pray a prayer of protection over your family today, using the name of Jesus.
verses.illustration_prompt: A disciple walking calmly along a path as a serpent curls away from their feet, light radiating from their sandals. Lush greenery, peaceful confidence. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Notwithstanding in this rejoice not, that the spirits are subject unto you; but rather rejoice, because your names are written in heaven.
verses.little_bible: But Jesus said: 'Don't get too excited just because you have power over evil spirits. The real reason to celebrate is this — your names are written in heaven!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Don't rejoice about power. Rejoice because your names are written in heaven!'
verses.meaning: Power and miracles can become sources of pride. The greatest gift is not what we can do but who we are — people known and saved by God.
verses.memory_phrase: Your name is written in heaven!
verses.prayer: God, thank You that You know my name and it is written in heaven. That is the best news ever!
verses.discussion_question: What is the most exciting piece of news you have ever heard? Is there anything more exciting than having your name in God's book?
verses.family_discussion: Why does Jesus want us to be more excited about salvation than about spiritual power? What does that teach us about what matters most?
verses.do_it_today: Write your name on paper and draw a star next to it. Remember: God knows that name and loves you.
verses.illustration_prompt: An open golden book in the sky with names being written by a shining hand, disciples far below looking up with wonder and joy. Heavenly watercolour light.

verses.kjv: In that hour Jesus rejoiced in spirit, and said, I thank thee, O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, that thou hast hid these things from the wise and prudent, and hast revealed them unto babes: even so, Father; for so it seemed good in thy sight.
verses.little_bible: Right then, Jesus was filled with joy in His Spirit and said: 'Thank You, Father — Lord of heaven and earth! You have hidden these things from people who think they are wise, and revealed them to little children. Yes, Father! This is exactly what pleased You!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus rejoiced and prayed: 'Father, You revealed Your truth to little children — not the proud and wise. Thank You!'
verses.meaning: This is one of the most extraordinary prayers in the Gospels. Jesus rejoices that God reveals truth to the humble and childlike rather than to the educated and proud. Children are not a disadvantage in receiving revelation — they are in a privileged position.
verses.memory_phrase: God loves revealing truth to children.
verses.prayer: Father, thank You that You love showing Your truth to me, even though I am young. I want to know You more.
verses.discussion_question: Do you think it is surprising that God reveals things to children that He hides from grown-ups? How does that make you feel?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus literally thanks God for revealing truth to 'babes' — this includes your children right now. How does that change the way you approach teaching your children about God?
verses.do_it_today: Tell a child near you: 'God loves revealing His truth to you. You can hear from Him!'
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing with face lifted joyfully to the sky, light pouring down on Him, small children gathered around His feet looking up with wide, wondering eyes. Radiant watercolour.

verses.kjv: All things are delivered to me of my Father: and no man knoweth who the Son is, but the Father; and who the Father is, but the Son, and he to whom the Son will reveal him.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'My Father has given Me everything. Only the Father truly knows who the Son is. And only the Son truly knows who the Father is — and those to whom the Son chooses to reveal Him.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Only God truly knows Me. And I reveal God to those I choose.'
verses.meaning: This verse shows the deep mystery and intimacy of the Trinity. Knowing the Father is only possible through Jesus — He is the one who opens the door to God.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus reveals the Father to us.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for showing us what the Father is like. Help me know God better through knowing You.
verses.discussion_question: What do you know about God the Father because of Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus is the only way to truly know God. How do we make knowing Jesus the foundation of our family's faith?
verses.do_it_today: Read a story of Jesus doing something kind today and say: 'That is what God the Father is like.'
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus and a father figure on a hilltop, one pointing to the other, with shafts of golden light connecting them. Below, small figures of people looking up with wonder. Luminous watercolour.

verses.kjv: And he turned him unto his disciples, and said privately, Blessed are the eyes which see the things that ye see:
verses.little_bible: Then Jesus turned privately to His disciples and said: 'How blessed you are — to see with your own eyes the things that you are seeing!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus told His disciples privately: 'You are so blessed to see what you are seeing!'
verses.meaning: The disciples were witnessing the arrival of God's kingdom in person. This was the moment all of history had been pointing toward, and they were right in the middle of it.
verses.memory_phrase: What a privilege to see Jesus!
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me see You clearly today — in Your Word, in prayer, and in the world around me.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to be truly blessed? Can you think of one way you are really blessed?
verses.family_discussion: We have the whole story of Jesus in the Bible. In what way are we also 'blessed eyes and ears' who get to know Him?
verses.do_it_today: Say out loud three things you are grateful to 'see' in your life because of Jesus.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus kneeling down to speak quietly with a small group of wide-eyed disciples in a garden, His voice low and intimate, the world carrying on behind them. Gentle watercolour.

verses.kjv: For I tell you, that many prophets and kings have desired to see those things which ye see, and have not seen them; and to hear those things which ye hear, and have not heard them.
verses.little_bible: Jesus continued: 'I tell you — many prophets and kings longed to see what you are seeing and never got to. They longed to hear what you are hearing and never did.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Great kings and prophets wanted to see what you see — and they never could!'
verses.meaning: From Abraham to David to Isaiah, the great figures of the Old Testament lived in hope of the Messiah. The disciples were living what they had only dreamed.
verses.memory_phrase: Kings and prophets longed for what we have.
verses.prayer: God, I have what the greatest people in the Bible longed for — the full story of Jesus. Help me not take that for granted.
verses.discussion_question: Can you name a king or prophet from the Bible? What do you think they were hoping to see?
verses.family_discussion: We have a completed Bible, the Holy Spirit, and the full revelation of Jesus. How do we honour that privilege?
verses.do_it_today: Read a prophecy from the Old Testament (like Isaiah 53) and see how Jesus fulfilled it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Ancient kings and prophets in robes looking up at the stars, reaching upward, as if hoping for something just beyond their view. A faint star in the distance. Wistful watercolour.

verses.kjv: And, behold, a certain lawyer stood up, and tempted him, saying, Master, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?
verses.little_bible: Just then a lawyer stood up to test Jesus. He asked: 'Teacher, what do I need to do to receive eternal life?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: A lawyer stood up and tried to trick Jesus: 'What do I do to get eternal life?'
verses.meaning: The lawyer did not ask sincerely — he asked to test Jesus. Yet Jesus turned the test into one of the greatest conversations in the Gospels.
verses.memory_phrase: Eternal life is the greatest question.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I want to know how to have life with You forever. Teach me.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think 'eternal life' means? Is it just living forever, or something more?
verses.family_discussion: The lawyer thought this question had a trick answer. Jesus thought it had a life-changing answer. How do we approach questions about God — with a testing heart or a seeking one?
verses.do_it_today: Ask a family member: 'If you could ask Jesus one question, what would it be?'
verses.illustration_prompt: A man in legal robes standing up confidently in a crowd, finger raised as if making a point, while Jesus sits calmly looking at him. The crowd watches curiously. Warm market scene watercolour.

verses.kjv: He said unto him, What is written in the law? how readest thou?
verses.little_bible: Jesus answered his question with a question: 'What does the law say? How do you read it?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus asked: 'What does God's law say? How do you read it?'
verses.meaning: Jesus did not lecture — He invited the lawyer to discover the answer from what he already knew. The truth was already in his hands; he needed to own it himself.
verses.memory_phrase: The answer is already in God's Word.
verses.prayer: God, help me not look for fancy answers when the truth is right there in Your Word.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever had a question that the answer was in a book — and you just had to read it?
verses.family_discussion: Why did Jesus ask the lawyer what he already knew? What does it teach us about how God deals with us?
verses.do_it_today: When you have a question about life today, try looking for the answer in the Bible first.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus gesturing gently back toward the lawyer with a calm smile, as if to say 'you already know this.' The lawyer looking a little surprised. Warm crowd around them.

verses.kjv: And he answering said, Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy strength, and with all thy mind; and thy neighbour as thyself.
verses.little_bible: The lawyer answered: 'Love the Lord your God with all your heart, all your soul, all your strength, and all your mind. And love the people around you as much as you love yourself.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Love God with everything — heart, soul, strength, and mind. And love others as you love yourself.
verses.meaning: The lawyer quoted the two greatest commandments — from Deuteronomy 6 and Leviticus 19. Together they sum up the whole law: love God completely, love people completely.
verses.memory_phrase: Love God. Love people. That is the whole law.
verses.prayer: God, help me love You with everything I have — and love people the way I love myself.
verses.discussion_question: Which is harder — loving God or loving other people? Why?
verses.family_discussion: What does it look like to love God with your MIND? With your STRENGTH? How can you love a neighbour as yourself this week?
verses.do_it_today: Do one loving thing for someone in your life today and think of it as loving God.
verses.illustration_prompt: The lawyer reciting the great commandments, both arms open wide, Jesus listening with a gentle nod. The words 'heart, soul, strength, mind, neighbour' written in the air around them in soft gold letters. Warm watercolour.

verses.kjv: And he said unto him, Thou hast answered right: do this, and thou shalt live.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'You have answered correctly! Do this — actually live it out — and you will have life.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Right answer! Now do it — and you will truly live.'
verses.meaning: Jesus confirmed the answer but shifted the emphasis from knowing to doing. Knowing the right answer is not enough — the challenge is living it.
verses.memory_phrase: Knowing is not enough — do it!
verses.prayer: God, help me not just know what is right but actually do it. Move my feet, not just my head.
verses.discussion_question: Do you know something that is right but find it hard to do? What stops you?
verses.family_discussion: There is a big gap between knowing God's commands and living them. How does your family close that gap?
verses.do_it_today: Pick one thing you know you should do — and do it today.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus with a firm but kind expression, pointing forward as if to say 'now go do it.' The lawyer looking suddenly less certain, more thoughtful. Open road in the background.

verses.kjv: But he, willing to justify himself, said unto Jesus, And who is my neighbour?
verses.little_bible: But the lawyer wanted to find a loophole. He asked: 'And who exactly counts as my neighbour?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The lawyer tried to find an excuse and asked: 'But who is my neighbour?'
verses.meaning: The question was designed to limit love — to find out who he did NOT have to love. Jesus' answer would completely reverse the question.
verses.memory_phrase: Love does not look for limits.
verses.prayer: God, forgive me when I look for excuses not to love people. Help me love without limits.
verses.discussion_question: Has anyone ever tried to find a rule that meant they didn't have to do something kind? What happened?
verses.family_discussion: The lawyer asked 'Who must I love?' but Jesus will change it to 'Who can I love?' How do those two questions lead to different lives?
verses.do_it_today: Instead of asking 'Do I have to help this person?' — ask 'How can I help this person?'
verses.illustration_prompt: The lawyer with a slightly clever look on his face, arms crossed, waiting for a loophole. Jesus looking at him with patient, knowing eyes. The crowd leaning in.

verses.kjv: And Jesus answering said, A certain man went down from Jerusalem to Jericho, and fell among thieves, which stripped him of his raiment, and wounded him, and departed, leaving him half dead.
verses.little_bible: Jesus told a story: 'A man was travelling from Jerusalem down to Jericho — a dangerous road. Thieves attacked him, stole everything he had, beat him up badly, and left him lying on the side of the road, nearly dead.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus told a story about a man attacked by thieves on a dangerous road. They beat him and left him half dead.
verses.meaning: The road from Jerusalem to Jericho was notoriously dangerous — steeply winding and known for robberies. Jesus placed His story in a real, recognisable setting. The man's condition was desperate.
verses.memory_phrase: A man lay helpless on the road.
verses.prayer: God, open my eyes to see people around me who are hurting and need help.
verses.discussion_question: Has anything really scary ever happened to you or someone you know that made you need help urgently?
verses.family_discussion: The story starts with a real person in real danger. How do we make sure we see real hurting people and don't treat them as problems to avoid?
verses.do_it_today: Think of one person you know who is going through something hard. How could you help them this week?
verses.illustration_prompt: A steep, winding dusty road through rocky hills, a lone man lying on the roadside, torn clothing, wounds on his arms and face, the road empty and quiet around him. Dramatic, compassionate watercolour.

verses.kjv: And by chance there came down a certain priest by that way: and when he saw him, he passed by on the other side.
verses.little_bible: A priest happened to come down that same road. He saw the man lying there — and walked to the other side of the road to avoid him.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: A priest came down the road, saw the hurt man — and crossed to the other side to avoid him.
verses.meaning: A priest was expected to be the most compassionate representative of God. Yet he walked past. Knowing religion is not the same as having a compassionate heart.
verses.memory_phrase: Don't cross the road to avoid someone hurting.
verses.prayer: God, help me never walk past someone who needs me. Give me a heart that stops.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think the priest walked past the hurt man instead of stopping?
verses.family_discussion: Sometimes we walk past people who need help — not out of cruelty, but out of fear, busyness, or not wanting to get involved. What stops your family from helping?
verses.do_it_today: The next time you see someone who looks sad or left out, stop and ask if they are okay.
verses.illustration_prompt: A robed priest hurrying down the opposite side of the dusty road, head slightly turned away from the injured man in the dirt, robes swishing. The hurt man's hand visible in the corner. Sobering watercolour.

verses.kjv: And likewise a Levite, when he was at the place, came and looked on him, and passed by on the other side.
verses.little_bible: Then a Levite came — another temple worker. He actually walked over to look at the man, then crossed the road and walked on anyway.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: A Levite came, looked at the hurt man — and also walked right past.
verses.meaning: The Levite came even closer before walking away, which may have made his choice worse. Seeing the need clearly and still choosing not to help is the definition of hardness of heart.
verses.memory_phrase: Seeing a need is not the same as meeting it.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me not just see needs but actually act. Don't let me be a looker who walks away.
verses.discussion_question: The Levite looked at the man but still walked away. Is that better or worse than not looking? Why?
verses.family_discussion: We often see needs around us and do nothing. What is the difference between sympathy (feeling sorry) and compassion (doing something)?
verses.do_it_today: If you notice a need today — act on it. Don't just notice and move on.
verses.illustration_prompt: A man in temple robes crouching briefly to peer at the injured man, then straightening and crossing the road, hands held behind his back. The hurt man's eyes watching him go. Quiet watercolour.

verses.kjv: But a certain Samaritan, as he journeyed, came where he was: and when he saw him, he had compassion on him,
verses.little_bible: But then a Samaritan man came travelling down the same road. When he saw the hurt man, his heart was moved with compassion — he felt deep care for him.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: A Samaritan man came and saw the hurt man. His heart was moved with compassion.
verses.meaning: Samaritans were despised by the Jewish people. The one who stopped was the one nobody expected — the outsider, the 'wrong' kind of person. Compassion crossed every boundary.
verses.memory_phrase: Compassion crosses every boundary.
verses.prayer: God, give me a heart that feels compassion — especially for people who are different from me.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever been surprised by a kind person who you didn't expect to help you?
verses.family_discussion: The hero of the story was the person Jews would have least expected. How does that challenge our assumptions about who is good and who is not?
verses.do_it_today: Do something kind today for someone who is different from you — different age, background, or personality.
verses.illustration_prompt: A Samaritan man in traveller's clothes stopping abruptly on the road, eyes wide with concern, looking down at the wounded man. His hand moving toward his heart. Warm, tender watercolour.

verses.kjv: And went to him, and bound up his wounds, pouring in oil and wine, and set him on his own beast, and brought him to an inn, and took care of him.
verses.little_bible: He went straight to the man and gently bandaged his wounds, pouring in oil and wine to clean and soothe them. Then he lifted him onto his own donkey, took him to an inn, and looked after him through the night.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: He bandaged the man's wounds, put him on his donkey, took him to an inn, and cared for him.
verses.meaning: The Samaritan's compassion was not just a feeling — it was total, hands-on, costly action. He gave his time, his supplies, his animal, and his personal attention.
verses.memory_phrase: Real compassion costs something.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me not just feel sorry for people but actually help them — even when it costs me something.
verses.discussion_question: The Samaritan used his own oil, wine, donkey, and time. What do you have that you could give to help someone?
verses.family_discussion: The Samaritan did not give money at arm's length — he got his hands dirty. What does hands-on compassion look like for your family?
verses.do_it_today: Do one hands-on kind thing today — something that costs you effort, not just coins.
verses.illustration_prompt: The Samaritan kneeling in the dirt beside the wounded man, carefully wrapping cloth around his wounds, a flask of oil beside him. Tender concentration on his face. Warm, compassionate watercolour.

verses.kjv: And on the morrow when he departed, he took out two pence, and gave them to the host, and said unto him, Take care of him; and whatsoever thou spendest more, when I come again, I will repay thee.
verses.little_bible: The next morning, when the Samaritan had to leave, he gave the innkeeper two silver coins and said: 'Please look after this man. If you spend more than this, I will pay you back when I return.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The next day, the Samaritan paid the innkeeper and said: 'Look after him. I'll pay you back anything extra when I return.'
verses.meaning: The Samaritan's generosity continued even after he had to move on — he made sure the man would be provided for and committed to return. This was complete, ongoing love.
verses.memory_phrase: Love goes all the way.
verses.prayer: God, help my kindness not stop halfway. Help me finish what I start when I try to help someone.
verses.discussion_question: Why did the Samaritan also leave money for the innkeeper? What does that show us about how he cared for the hurt man?
verses.family_discussion: The Samaritan planned ahead to make sure the man was fully cared for. What does long-term, committed care look like in your family — toward each other and toward others?
verses.do_it_today: Think of someone you helped once. Could you follow up on them and see how they are doing?
verses.illustration_prompt: The Samaritan pressing two silver coins into the innkeeper's hand, glancing back toward the doorway where the recovering man rests, with a warm promise on his face. Morning light. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Which now of these three, thinkest thou, was neighbour unto him that fell among the thieves?
verses.little_bible: Then Jesus asked the lawyer: 'Now — which of the three men do you think was truly a neighbour to the man who was attacked by thieves?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus asked: 'Which of the three was truly a neighbour to the hurt man?'
verses.meaning: Jesus completely reversed the original question. Instead of 'who is my neighbour?' — who do I have to love? — He asked 'who proved to be a neighbour?' — who did the loving?
verses.memory_phrase: Be a neighbour — don't just look for one.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me not ask 'who do I have to love?' Help me ask 'how can I love?'
verses.discussion_question: Jesus flipped the question! Instead of 'who is my neighbour?' He asked 'who WAS a neighbour?' What is the difference?
verses.family_discussion: The lawyer wanted to identify who deserved his love. Jesus changed it to: be the person who loves. How does that change the way we think about kindness?
verses.do_it_today: Instead of waiting for someone to be your friend, be a friend to someone today.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus with a knowing half-smile, gesturing back toward the road where the story just happened, while the lawyer's confident expression turns to one of genuine thought. Intimate watercolour scene.

verses.kjv: And he said, The one that shewed mercy on him. Then said Jesus unto him, Go, and do thou likewise.
verses.little_bible: The lawyer said: 'The one who showed him mercy.' Jesus replied: 'Yes. Now go and do the same.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The lawyer said: 'The one who showed mercy.' Jesus said: 'Go and do the same.'
verses.meaning: The lawyer could not even bring himself to say 'the Samaritan' — but Jesus' command was clear: go and be like that man. Mercy is the answer. Action is the test.
verses.memory_phrase: Go and show mercy.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me go and show mercy today. Not just feel it — show it.
verses.discussion_question: What is one way you could 'go and do likewise' — show mercy to someone — this week?
verses.family_discussion: This parable ends with a command, not just a lesson. What specific merciful act is your family going to do this week?
verses.do_it_today: Go and do one act of mercy for someone who needs it — right now if you can.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus pointing down the road ahead, the lawyer looking transformed — no longer smug, but sobered and ready to act. The road stretching forward into bright light. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Now it came to pass, as they went, that he entered into a certain village: and a certain woman named Martha received him into her house.
verses.little_bible: As Jesus and His disciples continued their journey, they came to a village. A woman named Martha welcomed Jesus into her home.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus came to a village. A woman named Martha welcomed Him into her home.
verses.meaning: Martha's hospitality was a beautiful act — she opened her home to Jesus and His disciples. The story that follows is not about hospitality being wrong, but about what takes priority.
verses.memory_phrase: Welcome Jesus into your home.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You are welcome in my heart and my home. Come and be with us.
verses.discussion_question: What do you do when a special guest comes to your house?
verses.family_discussion: Martha opened her home to Jesus. In what ways is your family's home open to Jesus — to His presence, His Word, His peace?
verses.do_it_today: Make your home feel welcoming today — tidy something, light something, or just make space for quiet.
verses.illustration_prompt: A warm, cheerful home with an open door, Martha standing in the doorway with a welcoming smile, Jesus and His disciples arriving along a village path. Warm late afternoon light. Watercolour.

verses.kjv: And she had a sister called Mary, which also sat at Jesus' feet, and heard his word.
verses.little_bible: Martha had a sister called Mary. Mary sat down at Jesus' feet and listened carefully to everything He said.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Mary, Martha's sister, sat at Jesus' feet and listened to every word He said.
verses.meaning: Sitting at a teacher's feet was a posture of discipleship — a student's position. Mary chose to be a student of Jesus, not just a host.
verses.memory_phrase: Mary sat and listened to Jesus.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me sit at Your feet like Mary and truly listen to You.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to really listen — not just hear, but really listen?
verses.family_discussion: Mary's posture was one of a learner. How does your family position yourselves as learners from Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Spend five minutes sitting quietly and reading or hearing one thing from the Bible. Just listen.
verses.illustration_prompt: Mary sitting on the floor at Jesus' feet, chin in her hands, eyes bright, completely absorbed in what He is saying. Jesus seated, speaking with warmth. Firelit room, soft colours.

verses.kjv: But Martha was cumbered about much serving, and came to him, and said, Lord, dost thou not care that my sister hath left me to serve alone? bid her therefore that she help me.
verses.little_bible: But Martha was rushing around, busy with all the work of preparing. She came to Jesus, frustrated, and said: 'Lord, don't You care that my sister has left me to do all the work by myself? Tell her to come and help me!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Martha was busy with all the work. She asked Jesus: 'Don't You care that Mary left me to do everything alone?'
verses.meaning: Martha's frustration was understandable — she was working hard. But she had become so consumed by serving Jesus that she had forgotten to be with Jesus.
verses.memory_phrase: Don't let busyness steal your time with Jesus.
verses.prayer: Lord, when I am very busy, help me still make time to sit with You. Don't let busyness crowd You out.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever felt like you were doing all the work and someone else wasn't helping? How did it feel?
verses.family_discussion: Martha was doing a good thing — serving. But it had become an anxious, resentful burden. How does busyness affect your family's peace and relationship with God?
verses.do_it_today: If you have been so busy that you haven't prayed today — stop, sit down, and talk to Jesus for five minutes.
verses.illustration_prompt: Martha in an apron with flour on her hands, standing in the kitchen doorway pointing back at her pots, a frustrated look on her face. Through the other doorway, Mary and Jesus visible in peaceful conversation. Warm home scene.

verses.kjv: And he said unto her, Martha, Martha, thou art careful and troubled about many things:
verses.little_bible: Jesus answered gently: 'Martha, Martha — you are worried and upset about so many things.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said gently: 'Martha, Martha — you are worried and troubled about so many things.'
verses.meaning: Jesus repeated her name — not with criticism, but with deep tenderness and care. He saw her anxiety and named it with love, not judgement.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus calls your name with love.
verses.prayer: Jesus, when I am worried and troubled, call my name gently like You called Martha's. Help me hear Your peace.
verses.discussion_question: When someone says your name twice in a kind, gentle way, what does it feel like?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus saw Martha's anxiety, not her failure. How do we speak to each other when someone in our family is anxious and overwhelmed?
verses.do_it_today: Speak to someone in your family or a friend today by name — gently and with care.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus looking at Martha with deeply kind, understanding eyes, his voice gentle. Martha's busy hands slowly stilling as she looks at Him, beginning to soften. Warm firelight, intimate moment.

verses.kjv: But one thing is needful: and Mary hath chosen that good part, which shall not be taken away from her.
verses.little_bible: Jesus continued: 'But only one thing is truly needed. Mary has chosen the best thing of all — and it will never be taken away from her.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Only one thing truly matters. Mary chose it — and no one can take it from her.'
verses.meaning: The 'one thing needful' is the presence of Jesus — hearing His word, being with Him. Everything else passes; this remains. Mary's choice could never be undone or lost.
verses.memory_phrase: Sitting with Jesus is the one thing needed.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me choose You — the one thing that truly matters. Help me sit at Your feet every day.
verses.discussion_question: If you could only have one thing — what would it be? What do you think Jesus would say if you chose Him?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus says only one thing is needful — being with Him. In our busy family life, how do we protect that one thing? What has to come first?
verses.do_it_today: Choose right now to sit quietly with Jesus — even for just five minutes. That is the one needful thing.
verses.illustration_prompt: Mary sitting peacefully at Jesus' feet in a pool of warm golden light, His hand resting gently near her, both at peace. Behind them, the noise of the kitchen is invisible and distant. Still, beautiful, luminous watercolour.
